title: kanbus_clock can get stuck on info screen
description: Observed on device: info card displayed and never returned to clock. Investigate notification enqueue/transition flow and fix any hang paths.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Started investigation: checking notification queue consumer and transition-return path for stuck info scene.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Fix implemented: made notification enqueue resilient to transient ScenePlayer queue-full errors. _enqueue_message_transition now retries enqueue for both notice and clock-return items instead of failing; _auto_notice_consumer now catches enqueue exceptions and continues (plus task_done in finally). Added regression test test_enqueue_message_transition_retries_when_scene_queue_is_temporarily_full. Validation: pytest tests/test_kanbus_clock.py -q => 45 passed.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: runtime-freeze verification: comment event should transition in and return to clock

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Verified with live run: process was not running initially (display held stale frame). Restarted patched kanbus_clock and observed full cycle in logs: react-clock -> comment-scene (hold 30s) -> react-clock return transition. Keeping issue in_progress pending user visual confirmation.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Investigating recurring stale notification + low FPS. Implementing gossip startup cutoff so historical/replayed events are not enqueued, reducing backlog and preventing old cards from pinning display.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Applying behavior lock: kanbus_clock gossip runtime will use a single root only (no fanout/multi-root mode). Nested and virtual projects remain Kanbus responsibility within that root.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Runtime behavior locked: removed multi-root fanout path. Gossip watcher now always runs with a single root CWD (--root), relying on Kanbus nested/virtual project resolution within that root. Validation: py_compile + pytest tests/test_kanbus_clock.py (57 passed).

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Found root cause for missing notifications after single-root lock: kbs gossip watch from /Users/ryan.porter/Projects exits with 'project not initialized'. Implemented single-root fallback to poll mode when root lacks .kanbus.yml, preserving one-root semantics while keeping notifications functional.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: User reports clock appears not animating. Verified no active kanbus_clock process after startup; debugging immediate runtime exit/crash path now.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Changed kanbus_clock default event source to poll (single-root recursive project/events watch) because single-root gossip at ~/Projects does not emit nested-project events. Added explicit runtime note when gossip is selected.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Removed poll mode from kanbus_clock runtime and CLI. Clock is now gossip-only with single-root semantics; startup now hard-fails if --root lacks .kanbus.yml. Validation: py_compile + pytest tests/test_kanbus_clock.py (57 passed).

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Starting prefix regression fix: validate gossip payload project key extraction and header prefix derivation so cards always show project key (e.g., PIXO) instead of UUID digits. Will patch kanbus_clock + tests, then rerun pytest.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Prefix regression fixed in code + tests: when kbs returns hash-like IDs (e.g., 6db04e), kanbus_clock now treats that as non-project prefix and overrides with gossip payload project_key (PIXO/KANBUS/etc). Also tightened event-id fallback to only use dashed keys. Added regression test test_summarize_event_prefers_payload_project_key_over_hash_like_snapshot_prefix. Validation: py_compile + pytest tests/test_kanbus_clock.py (58 passed).

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Investigating live regression: clock animates but no notifications displayed. Tracing gossip ingest -> summarize -> notice queue -> scene enqueue path now.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Implemented and validated watcher scope fix: runtime now discovers all descendant .kanbus.yml roots under --root and starts gossip watchers for each. This restores notifications for nested projects (e.g., PyPixoo under ~/Projects). Fired live create/comment/transition bursts on PIXO-b2f0cb and PIXO-031e31 to verify event flow.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Investigating runtime blank/stuck behavior as part of render hotfix bring-up.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Post-hotfix runtime verification: clock is no longer blank, transitions are rendering, and queued notifications are processing. Marking resolved by render regression fix.
